revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Aramatsuri-no-miya 02.jpg |name=Aramatsuri-no-miya |location={{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}} Ujitachicho | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 27 | lat_seconds = 22 | longitude = 136 | lon_minutes = 43 | lon_seconds = 30 |deity={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Major)
{{ill|Betsugū|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |established=Before {{ill|804|en|804|qid=Q29998|lt=804|1=804}} |main_hall={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |annual_festival= |label_position=left |map=Japan Mie |prefecture={{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.456111|N|136.725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Aramatsuri-no-miya''' (荒祭宮) is a {{ill|Betsugū|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated shrine) located within the precincts of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.{{#神宮便覧(大正14)|qq=}}p.30{{#平凡辞典1937一巻|qq=}}p.67 The enshrined deity is {{ill|Mitama|ja|天照坐皇大御神荒御魂|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami no Aramitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a manifestation of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}.{{#矢野、杜|qq=}}p.53-54 During the 62nd {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|qq=}} of Ise Jingū (2013), the {{ill|lt=relocation ceremony|Sengyo|ja|遷御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lt=relocation ceremony|qq=}} of Aramatsuri-no-miya took place at 8 PM on October 10, earlier than other Betsugū shrines.{{cite news | url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131014181310/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|title=Relocation Ceremony Held at Inner Shrine Betsugū | publisher={{ill|Yomiuri Shimbun|en|Yomiuri Shimbun|qid=Q645218|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}} |date=October 11, 2013 | accessdate=October 15, 2013|qq=}} == Overview == Aramatsuri-no-miya is located to the north of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Main Shrine (Naikū)|ja|正宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Shrine (Naikū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (御正宮) at Ise Jingū.{{三橋（2013）: 104ページ|qq=}}{{#伊勢年鑑(昭和17)|qq=}}p.22 The term "Betsugū" means "separate shrine,"{{#桜井1969学生伊勢|qq=}}p.164-165 and it is considered second only to the Main Shrine in sanctity.{{#神宮便覧(大正14)|qq=}}p.25 The name "Aramatsuri-no-miya" is generally interpreted to mean "shrine of the Aramitama (rough spirit)."{{#伊勢参宮案内|qq=}}p.30{{#広池国体|qq=}}p.77{{#高松宮日記2巻|qq=}}p.400 According to the "Jingū Zatsureishū," the shrine was established during the 26th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}'s reign (October) alongside the Main Shrine of Naikū.{{#皇大神宮史|qq=}}p.167-168 While {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, located outside the precincts, also enshrines Amaterasu's Aramitama, Aramatsuri-no-miya is regarded as the most prestigious among the ten Betsugū shrines of Naikū.{{#筑摩展開|qq=}}p.56-59{{#保育社カラー|qq=}}p.14-15 Its shrine building is larger than other Betsugū shrines, and it hosts the {{ill|Kanmisosai|ja|神御衣祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmisosai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493037|1=Kammiso-no-Matsuri}} in May and October, a ceremony not conducted at {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Outer Shrine).{{#筑摩展開|qq=}}p.100-102 == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Mitama|ja|天照坐皇大御神荒御魂|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami-no-Aramitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a manifestation of {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}'s {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q495568}}. The shrine is uniquely called "Aramatsuri-no-miya" (荒祭宮) rather than "Aramitama-no-miya," though the reason for this naming is unknown. The shrine's sacred object is a {{ill|Mirror|en|Mirror|qid=Q35197|lt=mirror|1=Mirror}}.{{#国史大系七|qq=}}p.262 It was first mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" (804), which described it as the "Aramitama Shrine, located 24 jō (approximately 72 meters) north of the Main Shrine." == Rituals == Rituals conducted at the Aramatsuri-no-Miya follow the practices of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, with offerings and quantities almost identical to those of the main shrine.Mitsuhashi (2013): p. 105 The festivals, including the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai|1=Kinensai}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kanname-sai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}, and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, receive {{ill|Heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heihaku|1=Hanpei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11481885}} (ritual offerings) from the Imperial Household. Imperial envoys, after attending the primary shrine, visit only the Aramatsuri-no-Miya among the {{ill|Betsugū|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Inner Shrine. The Kanmisosai (Ritual of Divine Garments), held in May and October, is unique to the Naikū and Aramatsuri-no-Miya.{{Cite book|和書|author=Katsunosuke Sakurai|title=伊勢神宮|publisher=学生社|year=1969|pages=140–141|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Kuniwo Kohori, Sadao Nichizuka|title=伊勢神宮|publisher=保育社|year=1996|pages=128–129|qq=}} The quantity of rice offerings for the Kanmisosai at Aramatsuri-no-Miya is approximately half that of the main shrine. During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|qq=}}, Aramatsuri-no-Miya is the only auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine to perform its relocation ceremonies almost simultaneously with the main shrine. Similarly, {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka-no-Miya|qq=}}, the auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, dedicated to the Aramitama (wild spirit) of its deity, receives the same treatment. == Shrine Structure == [[File:Aramatsuri-no-Miya 001.jpg|thumb|250px|New and old shrine buildings before the Shikinen Sengu]] Both the Aramatsuri-no-Miya (dedicated to the Aramitama of Amaterasu) and Taka-no-Miya (dedicated to the Aramitama of Toyouke) lack torii gates, though the reason remains unknown.{{Cite book|和書|author=Hideki Saito|title=読み替えられた日本神話|publisher=講談社|year=2006|pages=125–126|qq=}} The shrine building of Aramatsuri-no-Miya resembles the Inner Shrine, featuring {{ill|Chigi and Katsuogi|ja|千木・鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi and Katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q87220234}} in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style, thatched with kaya grass and facing south. The ancient site for the relocation (Shingoshikichi) is adjacent to the east and west. The shrine dimensions are defined as 6.42 meters in width (2 jō 1 shaku 2 sun), 4.24 meters in depth (1 jō 4 shaku), and 4.48 meters in height (1 jō 4 shaku 8 sun), making it larger than other auxiliary shrines. Originally, the shrine featured a sacred enclosure, a fire hall, and storehouses, but these were lost over time. Although the shrine experienced hardships during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, conditions improved slightly during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. By 1909, the shrine had been restored to a complete state. In 1936, the Aramatsuri-no-Miya building had to be temporarily relocated due to an infestation of bats. Notably, Prince Takamatsu (brother of Emperor Showa), intending to visit the shrine, described the series of misfortunes, including the {{ill|February 26 incident|en|February 26 incident|qid=Q477958|lt=February 26 Incident|1=February 26 incident}}, as "dreadful." The stone steps leading to the shrine, constructed during the Tenmei era (Edo period), were slightly altered in 1909. During this time, a decision was made to preserve a cedar tree midway up the steps.[[矢野、杜|矢野、伊勢神宮]] pp. 57–58 == Access == * '''Nearest station''': About 3 km from {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}}'s {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505724|1=Toba Line}} {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|qid=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}}. * '''Nearest bus stop''': Alight at the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} Naikū-mae bus stop. * '''Nearest interchange''': Approximately 2.1 km from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884857}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|qid=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}. * '''Parking''': Parking is available near the Inner Shrine, though it may be inaccessible during the New Year period. == References == == Sources == * "Oise Mairi," published by the Ise Jingu Reverence Association. *{{Cite book|和書|author=Masaki Ueda et al.|title=神宮の展開　伊勢の大神|publisher=筑摩書房|year=1988|isbn=4-480-85469-X|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=Kuniwo Kohori, Sadao Nichizuka|title=伊勢神宮|publisher=保育社|year=1996|isbn=4-586-50890-6|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=Hideki Saito|title=読み替えられた日本神話|publisher=講談社|year=2006|isbn=4-06-149871-1|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=Katsunosuke Sakurai|title=伊勢神宮|publisher=学生社|year=1969|isbn=4-311-40704-1|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=Prince Takamatsu|title=高松宮日記　第二巻|publisher=中央公論社|year=1995|isbn=4-12-403392-3|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=Kukai Nanri|title=決定版　伊勢の神宮|publisher=世界文化社|year=2014|isbn=978-4-418-14238-5|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=Kazuyoshi Miyoshi, Hirohiko Okano et al.|title=日本の古社　伊勢神宮|publisher=淡交社|year=2003|isbn=4-473-03108-X|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=Ken Mitsuhashi|title=伊勢神宮 日本人は何を祈ってきたのか|publisher=朝日新聞出版|year=2013|isbn=978-4-02-273516-4|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=Kenichi Yano|title=伊勢神宮　知られざる杜のうち|publisher=角川書店|year=2006|isbn=4-04-703402-9|qq=}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|qid=Q495568|lt=Aramitama and Nigimitama|1=Mitama}} * {{ill|List of the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of the 125 shrines of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}} * {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|qid=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} – A shrine also dedicated to the Aramitama of Amaterasu. Its enshrined deity is recorded as "Tsu-no-Sakaki-Sugomi-no-Mikoto-Ama-Kushitama-no-Hime-no-Mikoto." In 1956, it received the former shrine building of the Aramatsuri-no-Miya. * {{ill|Seoritsu Hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} * [http://www.isejingu.or.jp/naiku_2.html Aramatsuri-no-Miya - Ise Jingu] * [http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/index.html Hirota Shrine - Amaterasu Aramitama] {{Jingu125|qq=}} {{Shinto horizontal|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|荒祭宮|version=99094503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Aramatsuri-no-miya}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Aramatsuri-no-Miya (Q2859485)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|qid=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|lt=Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮|commons|Category:Aramatsuri-no-miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|fr|Aramatsuri no miya|qid=Q2859485|1=Aramatsuri-no-Miya}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|qid=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|qid=Q135009132|lt=Tsukinami|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}}{{efn|gets offerings for the yearly {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} and the monthly {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}.}}) || {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|qid=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.456135|136.725128|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181109 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q2859485}} [[da:d:Q2859485]] [[es:Aramatsuri no miya]] [[fr:Aramatsuri no miya]] [[ja:荒祭宮]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]]==Notes== [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 2013]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]]
